title: crassula ruamahanga
intro: Crassula ruamahanga, also known as the New Zealand jade or the necklace stonecrop, is a succulent plant species native to New Zealand. Traditionally, this plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Crassula ruamahanga include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a salve, ointment, or infused oil, which can be applied topically to the affected area.
